title: festuca ovina var. paludosa
intro: Festuca ovina var. paludosa, also known as marsh fescue, is a type of grass that is native to certain regions of the world, particularly in mountainous and wetland areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of marsh fescue include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Marsh fescue can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
